About Todd Brown
Considered the #1 authority on engineering profitable customer acquisition campaigns, Todd Brown is the marketing expert other marketing experts go to when they need help with their own business.

With clients in over 71 different countries, operating in over 65 different mass and niche markets, it’s been said that Todd has helped his clients and students engineer more six and seven-figure marketing campaigns than any other expert online today.

His list of coaching students, consulting clients, and subscribers reads like a Who’s Who of A-List Entrepreneurs.

His Agency has created the promotions behind some of the biggest direct response marketers and companies online today.

And, entrepreneurs fly-in to West Palm Beach, Florida — from all around the globe — for a single day of guidance with Todd to implement his renowned E5 Method.

web.markdown:
![Thumbnail (1920x1080)](https://i.ytimg.com/vi/6LLfLkNXhsk/maxresdefault.jpg)
# [How Pro Marketers Make Funnels Scalable](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6LLfLkNXhsk)

**Visibility**: Public
**Uploaded by**: [Todd Brown](http://www.youtube.com/@ToddBrownMarketing)
**Uploaded at**: 2024-05-14T15:08:42-07:00
**Published at**: 2024-05-14T15:08:42-07:00
**Length**: 25:46
**Views**: 14219
**Likes**: 294
**Category**: Howto & Style

## Description

```
Todd Brown shares a full training on the key differences between the way pro marketers and the average approach optimizing marketing funnels for scalability. 

If you're an online marketer or entrepreneur and you want to understand how to get your funnel to bring you more new buyers, customers, clients, members, etc., this will be one of the most important marketing lessons you learn. 

***
For more information on Todd's TOP ONE™ Mastermind Group, go here:
http://TopOneMastermind.com
```

## Transcript

hey Todd Brown here so in this video I'm going to share with you one of the absolute most valuable lessons that you can learn if you're an online marketer or entrepreneur and you're looking to scale your new customer acquisition if you're looking to optimize your funnels to be able to get more and more new buyers new clients new customers from Paid media because what I'm going to walk you through are the specific differences between the pro marketer and the average typical marketer in What's Done in terms of op optimizing a funnel for scale in other words what the pro marketers do when it comes to optimizing a funnel for scale is wildly different than what the average marketers do the average marketers approach it from a completely different perspective you could go as far as to say and you're going to see in this training in this video that the average marketers are playing a wildly different game when it comes to optimizing their funnels compared to the way that Pros optimize their funnels to be able to scale with paid media so let's go over to the computer and I'm gonna walk you step by step through the exact differences in how Pros compared to average optimize funnels for scale all right so here's what we're going to do we're going to go average what the average do and then what the pro does what the average do and then what the pro does so that you can see the difference specific differences here so the very first thing that you'll see here is that the average marketer the average entrepreneur thinks when it comes to optimizing a funnel for scale they think of optimization merely as getting a higher sales conversion rate or lowering the cost of traffic and this is why you see the typical marketer when they think of optimizing a campaign or when they think of fixing a broken campaign a campaign that's not producing well that's not bringing in enough sales what they immediately think about and where they immediately go to is I need to increase sales conversion rate I either I need to pay less for my traffic and or I need to increase my sales conversion rate well this is wildly different from the way the pro marketer thinks about it because Pros recognize and this is the Crux of this lesson for you this is something that I drill into my top one Mastermind clients all the time this is a focus of what it is that we do the pro recognizes that optimizing a campaign for scale to get more and more buyers from Paid traffic is a game of closing the gap between CPA and aov so what is CPA it is cost per acquisition that is how much does it cost you to acquire a new buyer a new client a new student a new subscriber and aov is the other side of the equation it's the value side of the equation aov is average order value that's the average amount of money that a new buyer client subscriber student spends with you so CPA is what does it cost you to get that buyer and aov is how much does that buyer spend with you see the pro recognizes that what determines whether a campaign is scalable or not and how scalable that campaign is with paid media comes down to this one ratio and this is why I refer to the this ratio CPA to aov as the golden ratio because at the end of the day what determines whether you can scale the campaign is the CPA to aov ratio how much does it cost you to get a buyer and how much is that buyer worth sales conversion rate does not tell you whether the campaign is scalable or not you can have a 10% sales conversion rate and that does not mean that you have a scalable campaign you could also have a 1% sales conversion rate and that doesn't mean that it's not a scalable campaign you cannot deposit sales conversion rate what you do deposit is the difference between the CPA and aov right if it cost you $100 to acquire a buyer and every buyer spends $100 with you that means you're acquiring buyers for free you're acquiring the single most valuable asset in your business for free and this is why first and foremost Pros recognize that the entire game of scaling and optimizing for scale comes down to making that CPA to aov ratio work that's the number that determines scalability not sales conversion rate not the other marketing performance metrics which we're going to talk about so that's the first point of difference next is that the average focus on increasing the sales conversion rate of their core offer Andor lowering their traffic cost pretty much the same thing that I just said right they focus on they think first and foremost that optimization is all about get cheaper traffic cheaper traffic cheaper traffic and convert more convert more convert more and so they focus on trying to optimize through increasing sales conversion rate or lowering their traffic cost somehow but the pros focus on driving up aov driving up the average amount of money that the typical buyer spends the day that they become a buyer and in addition to that the pros focus on driving up life time value now lifetime value we can look at lifetime value or incremental customer value at 30 days after they purchase 60 days 90 days 6 months 9 months and certainly lifetime see the point here is that Pros recognize that and focus on building up the value of the buyer building up the amount of money that a buyer spends the day that they become a buyer and with every additional future transaction they focus on the value side of the equation whereas the average by focusing on sales conversion rate and lowering traffic cost are focusing on trying to lower the cost side of the equation see if you look at this on the left we've got the cost CPA on the right we've got the value which is aov and lifetime value the average is focusing on trying to bring down the cost see when you increase sales conversion rate if all else remains equal what you're doing is you're driving down the cost to acquire a buyer of course if you lower your traffic cost by putting out better creative better targeting or finding a cheaper Source or something like that of traffic what are you doing you're if all else remains equal you're lowering your cost see the average are focusing on the cost side of the equation the pros are focusing on the value side of the equation see you've got to realize as well that there's only so much that you can lower your cost right there's only so much if if you're paying $100 to um to acquire a buyer you only have a $100 window that you can swing and you're never going to bring the cost down to zero anyway right but you only have a $100 window but on the value side there is an infinite upside there is no limit to how much you can increase the value of a buyer right and and and so it's important to recognize the average are focusing on the cost side and the pros are focusing on the um the value side the other thing to really recognize and why this is critically important for you is because you've got to recognize that in sophisticated markets in mature markets in almost every Market over time the costs to acquire a buyer go up as more and more competitors are buying media right that means that uh click-through rates go down when click-through rates go down that means the cost to generate a visitor goes up which if you're generating leads the cost to generate a lead will go up the cost to acquire a buyer will go up right that things traffic generation becomes more expensive over time and so if you're trying to optimize a campaign by fighting against that natural increase in costs you're man you're you're going head-to-head with the natural trend of a market the healthier smarter way to go about it is to realize that costs over time are going to to go up and how do you compensate for that you don't compensate like the average do by trying to keep cost down constantly you compensate for that by focusing on the aov and lifetime value by focusing on increasing the amount of money that the average customer spends with you the day that they become a customer and through all future transactions that's the second difference in the way the pros um optimize campaigns for scale compared to the average next next is the average they think that the most important aspect of an optimized campaign is a high enough sales conversion rate and I know we're focused on sales conversion rate right now but this is it's really important because I want you to ask yourself if this is how you're thinking and this is how you're operating they're constantly thinking like they're constantly saying well what's the sales conversion rate that I should shoot for what's the what's a good enough sales conversion rate or what's a good sales conversion rate and here's the reality I really don't care what the sales conversion rate is right like we're going to talk about how we use sales conversion rate in a specific context and what it's used for in just a little bit but I want you to understand that I don't care what the sales conversion rate is if my CPA to aov ratio is where I need it to be because whether I have a 10% sales conversion rate or a 1% sales conver conversion rate does not determine whether I have a viable scalable campaign what does deter in that is the CPA to aov ratio and so while the average is focused relentlessly on is this a high enough sales conversion rate what should my sales conversion rate be um is this too low of a sales conversion rate how do I increase sales conversion rate the pros they're not shooting for a Target sales conversion rate we're not aiming at a we're not aiming for a specific 3% or 4% or 10% or 12% Pros are shooting for a Target aov and or LTV right see if I know that it costs $120 to acquire a buyer or $150 to acquire a buyer or $200 to acquire a buyer what I'm thinking about is not sales conversion rate I'm thinking about how do I make my aov 120 150 200 220 250 300 the reality is is that if your aov is high enough it does not matter what your sales conversion rate is yes of of course there's value in lowering the sales conversion rate excuse me increasing sales conversion rate because it lowers your customer acquisition costs yes there's value there but lowering excuse me increasing sales conversion rate to lower your customer acquisition cost is a much more difficult game than is increasing aov first and foremost right when you are working on increasing sales conversion rate you're working with prospects we're talking about getting more prospects more non buyers to buy when we're talking about working on aov and increasing aov we're talking about working with buyers and it's a lot easier to get buyers to buy more from you than it is to get more prospects to buy from you so while the average are focused on is my sales conversion rate high enough Pros are focused on is my aov and or lifetime value high enough major Point third Point next next is the average the average is constantly the average marketer entrepreneur is focused on campaign performance metrics campaign performance metrics are things like optin rate sales conversion rate um order form completion rate bump offer take rate upsell take rate or bump offer and upsell um conversion rate all of those things tell you how each piece of a campaign is performing but none of those things tell you whether a campaign is viable or not whether it's scalable or not and how scalable it is and this is why Pros focus on campaign Financial metrics not campaign performance metrics but campaign Financial metrics like cost per acquisition like average order value like customer value at X number of weeks or months like lifetime customer value because those things the excuse me the cost side and the value side are are ultimately what you can deposit right it doesn't matter what your sales conversion rate is or your optin rate is or your upsell take rate is none of those things matter if your CPA to aov ratio is Off the Mark right none of those things matter you can't deposit sales conversion rate opt-in rate order form completion rate upsell take rate bump offer take rate you can't deposit any of those things what you deposit are the financial metrics or based on the financial metrics and so while average marketers are enamored with these performance metrics and they're constantly kind of in this marketer mindset the pros are focused on the campaign Financial metrics and monitoring and manipulating and increasing those things to get within kpi and this is something again that I drill into our top one Mastermind folks this is kind of a mindset shift that I kind of drill into them constantly keeping them in this this different mindset which we're actually going to unpack in in in just a bit as well and so next is the average they focus on trying to increase aov with maybe one two maybe three TR transaction maximization offers transaction maximization offers are all of those different types of offers like a bump offer an upsell a down sell a second chance offer um an immediate backend a credit upgrade all these things that you know that we our folks that maybe I'll put into a later uh video for you they'll focus on maybe one or two of those things whereas the pros they focus on trying to increase aov with as many different transaction maximization offers as possible whether it is a self-liquidating offer an upgrade offer bump offer upsell down sell Second Chance postale credit offer immediate backend all those things that I that I talked about see the pro recognizes that every additional dollar that we add to AO V gives us more leverage and greater ability to drive more traffic to pay more for traffic right and so while while right the average marketer is using maybe one upsell or two upsells and they've got very little followup uh for a second chance offer for a postale credit offer U and whatnot because they're so they because they think that the optimization comes from sales conversion rate Pros are just wildly focused on what are all of the different levers that we can pull to increase average order value because if the average order value is high enough you can drive oh you can drive traffic out the wazo you can pay more for traffic than your competitors you can use the worst sources of traffic that your competitors can't use because your aov is higher meaning that your customers are spending more with you your customers are worth more and when your customers are worth more you can spend more to acquire those customers you can when your aov is high enough you can have much lower per forming marketing campaigns right if your aov is high enough right you can run campaigns that have a 1% sales conversion rate or a one and a half sales uh percent sales conversion rate right and still have a scalable campaign right and still have a scalable campaign because what determines scalability a CPA to aov ratio and so the difference is is that the pros are looking for all of the different points to drive up aov they're looking for all of the different levers of aov contrib contribution next is the average they focus on trying to make the numbers work right away with a single campaign in other words what I want you to understand is that is that ultimately the average they'll take one campaign that they're using to acquire new buyers and they'll often just look at that one campaign maybe they'll look over a three-day window or a two-day window or a five-day window of what that campaign produced and they'll look at the costs and they'll look at the revenue generated over those 3 days or two days or five days of that one campaign and then they'll make a decision about whether it is a viable campaign or not often the pros they're willing to have a much longer timeline to break even to generate profit so often times they may use a longer timeline and even use their first backend offer another in other words a second followup campaign to buyers uh as a way to make the numbers work you know I'll never forget let me kind of share a story with you from uh Clayton make piece one of my one of my early mentors great dude may he rest in peace and legendary copywriter I'll never forget he shared this story with me about when he was with Weiss Financial and they looked at the numbers and I'll probably bang up the story a little bit but let me tell you this very quickly because it shows exactly what we're talking about here they had worked on a campaign and when they looked at the numbers at the end of 30 days right 30 days after uh um you know campaign plus 30 days the owner you know Martin Weiss said to him this campaign the numbers just don't work and so we've got to can this campaign and Clayton reviewed the numbers and he said to Martin he said I have I have the fix I there is one little thing that we can do that make these numbers work very very simple very easy right away and will be profitable and Martin said great love it what is it Clayton said instead of looking at the numbers at 60 days let's look at the number at 45 days and at 45 days right it they were profitable and the reason was because between day 30 and day 45 they had another campaign that got rolled out to further monetize those folks and so and this is important and so right like at the end of the day and we're going to we're going to come back to this it's the difference between the way that the average marketer thinks which I'm going to tell you about in in in just a second and the way that the pro thinks hold on to that because we're going to come back to it wildly valuable was a GameChanger for me and I think it's going to be for you as well the average marketer cares most about how well their campaign performs their funnel performs meaning they're they're so caught up in how well is this opt-in page performing what's the opt-in rate how well is this vssl or long form sales or webinar performing how well is it converting right how many appointments is it driving or how many buyers how many people is it driving to the um the order form they are so focused and care most about how well the different pieces and parts of their campaign perform whereas the pros they care most about the campaign Financial metrics and how they perform see I'm not married or tied to or have my identity linked to how uh how well the optin page works like what the optin rate is or what the sales conversion rate is or what the order form abandonment rate is or what the upsell take rate is or what the bump offer take rate is right I don't care most about those I care most about the financial metrics most specifically on the front end when it comes to acquisition the CPA to aov ratio that's everything I don't if the CPA to aov ratio is where I want it to be let's just say hypothetically all we're looking to do on the front end is Break Even meaning that for every dollar we invest we make a dollar back meaning that right we're acquiring new customers for free our bank account is no less today than it was uh days ago right if that's what we're looking for and we run a new campaign and out of the gate we've got a one: one CPA to aov ratio for every dollar that we invest right we make a dollar back plus get customers and leads I am thrilled I do not care what the optin rate is registration rate is sales conversion rate is order form abandonment rate is I don't care about any of those things why because what matters is the CPA to aov ratio campaign Financial metrics I don't care if I have a 1% sales conversion rate I don't care if we have a uh an 80% order form abandonment rate I don't care if we have a 15% um upsell conversion rate if I have a one: one ratio CPA to aov I'm thrilled because I have a scalable campaign I have a campaign that allows me to bring in buyers with zero cost my bank account is no less today than it was days ago but now I'm acquiring the single most valuable asset in my business which is customers which then we're going to put into the back end and deliver more value to them and so really important and then finally the last difference goes to that mindset the mindset that I mentioned just a a a moment ago and that is the average marketer the average entrepreneur when it comes to optimizing their campaigns for scale they think like a marketer in other words they're they're thinking like a a a marketer they're thinking about the marketing they're thinking about the marketing performance whereas Pros are thinking like investors see I want to leave you with this that right and this is another thing that I drill into our top one Mastermind folk this is a big shift that I make with them for them and I help them maintain this is that is that look we're really just investors as entrepreneurs we're investing in the acquisition of assets just like a real estate investor is investing in the acquis I of real estate assets real estate property we are doing the same thing as entrepreneurs and as online marketers with our own with our own offers and products and services we're investing in the acquisition of assets those assets are customers or clients or subscribers or members what or patients whatever it may be right and those assets those customers clients students subscribers ET have a cost just like with real estate when you're investing in a real estate asset there's a cost to acquire that those assets our assets have a cost when we're using paid media we're looking to scale they have a cost and then they have a value to us on day one the day or day Zero we call it internally which is the day that they become a buyer so there's a cost to acquire the asset there's a value money that goes back in our pocket the day that we acquire that which is what is that right there CPA aov and then there is a future value there is lifetime value there is right assuming that we've got back-end offers which is what this business is all about a direct response-driven business is all about the back end the back end is the real business that we are in that ultimately we know that these people are going to get more value uh from us by participating in more transactions so we're going to generate more money and so on and so the value of a customer is only going to go up right after that initial initial transaction and so right we're just investors that's all we're doing right we're we're looking at the financials of what does it cost us to acquire this asset what does this asset put in our put back in our pocket the day that we acquire it and then how much more does it put in our pocket over the next coming weeks and months think about Netflix right Netflix right they know how much it costs to acquire a subscriber they know what that subscriber is worth the day that they become a subscriber whatever the average monthly fee is for Netflix and then because they know how long the average subscriber is going to stay a subscriber with them they know the average lifetime value of of a subscriber so then it just became becomes a game of math investing is a game of arithmetic of math and that's why the pros stay focused on the financials the math the the the the financial math side instead of the marketing performance side if you can just as one simple example if you can acquire new buyers with a CPA to aov ratio of one to one and you have a back end sky is the limit for you because you are you are and can acquire new buyers for free and then you put them into your back end and that's where all the profit is made and that's where right that's where you make bank and so I want you to think about what it is that I shared with you in this training there's a tremendous difference between the way the pros think and operate and approach optimizing a campaign for scale compared to the way the the average do and so there you go the difference between the pro and the average when it comes to optimizing funnels for scale talk to you in the next video

# [How To Scale Your Funnel Using Math & Paid Traffic | Todd Brown](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mi52WoThZhM)

**Visibility**: Public
**Uploaded by**: [Anik Singal](http://www.youtube.com/@AnikSingal)
**Uploaded at**: 2022-10-03T04:00:22-07:00
**Published at**: 2022-10-03T04:00:22-07:00
**Length**: 46:28
**Views**: 2950
**Likes**: 118
**Category**: People & Blogs

## Description

```
How do you scale your funnels for your information products with paid ads?😀

That’s the exact question Todd Brown answered for me today! But we went a step further, we also discussed whether this is something you should even do with YOUR business.

A lot of people just jump into paid ads without seeing the whole “bigger picture.” 

We addressed that today!

Todd also covered…

✅ The specific math to learn when it comes to scaling your business…
✅ The single biggest thing most people get wrong about buying traffic…
✅ Whether you actually NEED to scale your business…

And don’t forget to follow Todd at http://e5bundle.com👇


⏱ Chapters
0:00 Intro
1:09 My Background With Todd
6:50 Where Todd Starts When Scaling Businesses
14:38 Example: Inside the Math of Scaling a Business
18:58 Learning to Trust the Data
28:10 What to Consider Before Scaling Your Traffic
35:02 Is Scaling Right For You?
40:41 Todd’s “E5” Method 
43:41 Outro


============================================

👇Subscribe To The Channel By Clicking Below!👇
https://www.youtube.com/user/aniksingalcom?sub_confirmation=1

CHECK OUT THESE TOP TRENDING PLAYLISTS NOW!

Facebook Advertising Strategies - 
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BMQh6zA3HUY&list=PLEmF7qw7SECJUULNlnAGHvcegeQbIAHZp

How To Become A Better Entrepreneur - https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLEmF7qw7SECKVlP2eOsF_XpYBYhlTGAVU

============================================

“Lead Fighter” — That’s the title Anik Singal gives himself as a high-energy, trailblazing Entrepreneur.

Anik got his start in the online scene back in 2003 from his college dorm room.

Ever since then he’s gone on to build 6 successful companies, launched 22 top brands, generated over $250 Million in sales, and taught over 250,000 students worldwide - how to start, grow, and scale a successful online business.

As the founder of Lurn, Inc., 

Anik Singal’s passion is in creating dynamic online classroom environments that teach people how to enhance their business, financial, and personal lives. 

Anik Singal has become a go-to authority in the areas of...

✅Digital Publishing.
✅Event-Based Marketing.
✅Product Launches.
✅Email Marketing.

Anik has been voted one of the Top 3 Young Entrepreneurs by BusinessWeek Magazine. In addition, his company earned the prestigious Inc. 500 Fastest Growing Companies in America two years in a row.

All of Anik’s experiences have made him the person he is today…

From struggling for 18 months when he first started, then successfully building his business to over $10 Million a year.

Then losing it all and falling to $1.7 Million in debt and almost declaring bankruptcy. 

Bouncing back and generating over $10 million in 16 months, paying back all of his debt and he hasn’t looked back since.

He’s worked with and has been endorsed by some of the most influential Entrepreneurs of our time... 

Including Robert Kiyosaki, Les Brown, Daymond John, Bob Proctor, Grant Cardone, and many more.

Anik is a dreamer. A thinker. A fighter. Most importantly, Anik is a teacher.

His immediate goal is empowering 1 Million Entrepreneurs to live the life of their dreams by the end of 2019.

they're supposed to be performing but the cpa to aov ratio is off then we have to ask well is it a media performance issue or is it a pricing issue what do i mean by media performance issue i mean that is your media buying your ads are they performing the way that they're supposed to be performing right are your you know so let me let me simply say right we'll say this are we paying more for traffic than we should be right is ultimately what we want to look at like are we paying more for traffic than what that traffic is actually worth in the marketplace and so how do we assess that well we just look at two things really number one is are we targeting the correct audience right so some of this of course is this is when i said earlier nuance is subjective uh to some extent right i know i said we want to move away from subjectivity as much as we can we're never going to move completely away from some subjective decisions but are we targeting the right audience so are we right are we putting our ads out in front of the correct audience and are our ads getting the click-through rate that they need to be getting or we're looking to see if any one of these are off the mark why are we looking why i want you to understand the context of this right we're looking to see are we targeting the right people and if we believe that we're targeting the right people are our ads getting a reasonable click-through rate uh um because right ultimately what we're trying to see is we're trying to see if we're paying a lot for a visitor remember in that example i said what did i say we were paying ten dollars a visitor right we're paying ten dollars a visit let me just go back real quick to this bad boy right over here so i said we were paying ten dollars a visitor so um over here right we have a cpa of 250. well okay so why why is our cpa so high right well we're paying ten dollars a a a visitor that's an economic issue okay so is it a media performance issue meaning are we paying ten dollars a visitor because our media stinks our our creative our images our our headline our copy or is it a pricing issue which i'll explain in a second so in order to look at our media i'm going to look at are we targeting the right people and are we um getting is our click-through rate where it needs to be so if the answer is yes like yeah we're targeting we're we believe that we're targeting the right people we've always targeted these people and our click-through rate is reasonable then what i here's what i want you to understand i want you to i want you to get this context and i want you to listen to me carefully we've we've arrived at a point where we we may be dealing with just an escalating cost of media an escalating cost of traffic in other words recognize this we've got a campaign that has assets in the example that i showed you that are performing well we've got a good sales conversion uh a good sales conversion rate we've got a good bump offer take rate we've got a good upsell one take rate we've got a good upsell to take take great those prices are are are right like the performance of our marketing assets is good the performance of our media is good we're targeting the right people our click-through rates are are are good so look from a pure performance perspective right from a pure performance perspective all of our assets on the media side and on the funnel side are performing the way that they are supposed to but yet we are still paying an arm and a leg for a visitor at this point we i lean towards all right so now what we have to do is we have to be able to afford the escalating cost of media now we have to be able to pay for that higher cost of media because we've we're doing a good job on the media side in terms of uh targeting in terms of click-through rate click-through rate right if the click-through rate is there is is where it's supposed to be that means our creative is good right our our funnel assets our sales page you know our marketing and sales page is working well our bump offer is working well our upsell is working well our upsell number two is working well right and so those things are all working well yet the price is still high for a visitor here is where what we would do is we would test offer pricing we would test the price on offers knowing that right that as you test a higher price point that sales conversion rate could come down that's okay there is a point where right you can raise price sales conversion rate could come down raise price and it'll make the numbers work like if the numbers don't work if aov cpa doesn't doesn't work right for you what that means ultimately is that you don't have a viable campaign so our first order of priority to have a viable campaign to make it gold is to get that ratio it's not about i want to hold on to my 10 sales conversion rate who cares if you have a 10 sales conversion rate but your aov and cpa ratio is off the mark therefore you you can't you can't run with it who cares what the sales conversion rate is what you should care about the whole game the whole deciding factor is what are we investing and what is it worth to us what's the cpa and what is the aov so here we test offer pricing and where i recommend you start this offer pricing is on the right side and so meaning i would start by testing the price on upsell number one then i would go to upsell number two then i would go to your bump and then finally i would test the price on your core offer why do we save the the price testing on the core offer for last because the price of the core offer has a direct has a direct connection to sales conversion rate so ultimately i know if we doubled or tripled let's call let's say we doubled the price on the core offer tripled the price whatever well our sales conversion rate is going to come down and with a front-end campaign that means that we're acquiring less buyers so i want to save the core offer and testing price on that offer for last so that we can leave the conversion rate uh where it is right now right where it is and i want to see if we can make up the deficit by changing the pricing on the other side of the equation so we're not playing around with sales conversion rate or we're not impacting sales conversion rate we're impacting the value of the buyer through aov right through through the other side through the transaction maximization um offers and so i hope that makes sense for everybody if you've got a question for that we're gonna i'm to be wrapping up in about 10 minutes and we'll we'll we'll uh you can fire that away so we start with we we start with the upsell upsell number one and then go to right upsell two if you've got an upsell to and then the bump offer the reason also why we save the price testing on the um on the bump offer until after the upsells is because that can have a direct impact on order form completion which can also have an impact on overall sales conversion rate and cpa so i want to try to to uh to make it work by price testing on the upsell number one first which is the biggie in the campaign then upsell two then go back to the bump and then the core um the core offer and i want you to remember here like i said already that what we are looking for here what we are looking for here is aov contribution what i mean by that is that if we tested let's say we at 199 we have a 10 sales conversion rate and we raised the price to 399 just as an example we go from 199 to 399 and our sales conversion rate comes down two percent well if that 399 at eight percent sales conversion rate is contributing more to average order value than is the 199 at 10 that's the one that we're gonna go with we're gonna go with the one that contributes the most amount to aov we're gonna go with the one in other words performance you decide whether a price test on with the upsell one upsell two is effective is successful when it contributes more money to aov it is not determined by how much it increases conversion rate it is determined by how much it increases aov how much it contributes to aov and so to keep cruising here is an example of what i would consider not an economic issue so let's say we're paying four dollars a visitor meaning we get 100 visitors it costs us 400 bucks now to get those um 100 visitors let's say we've got the same exact uh setup here uh in terms of our offers but we got a four percent sales conversion rate on the core offer still we've got a ten percent now uh take rate on the bump offer we got an eight percent uh take rate on upsell one and we got a three percent take rate on upsell two well this is the same for buyers here we've got a cpa of 100. you could see here right just as an example that our cost of media which is something i said to you guys early on our cost of media dropped in half uh uh right our cost drop no it dropped more than in half it dropped from ten dollars to to uh to four dollars here and you could and we acquired the same amount of buyers you could see here that so our cpa dropped substantially in the prior example it was 250 here it is um it is 100 right we spent 400 we got four buyers that's a 100 cost per acquisition here in this case we have an aov of 88.73 we are still at a 21.27 deficit but this is what i want you to notice compared to the uh the prior example is that this is not an economic issue at this point because we have marketing assets that are underperforming so we've got an you know upsell number one that's you know only at eight percent we've got upsell number two that's only at three percent we've got an underperforming bump offer um here let's say or you know whatever a subpar bump offer meaning that there is a great likelihood that we can through dialing in uh one or more of these assets we can hit our aov to cpa ratio right maybe we could get it to break even let's say in this example this is an example of right the reason why there's a 21.27 deficit here it's because we have one or more marketing assets that are under performing and so this is not an economic issue this is a performance issue this is about performance with uh you know with one or more of our um campaigns and so what do we do here well we in in fancy language we identify the earliest campaign constraint you could maybe make the case in meaning the constraint is the earliest step that is underperforming where it should be so maybe we make the case that it's this bad boy and so my point in sharing this with you is that what we're doing through this line of questioning and through this assessment is we are narrowing down what is the cause of our cpa to aov ratio being off if it is an economic issue we're going to look at well is it because of the performance of our media like we're targeting the wrong people our click-through rate is off if we're targeting the right people and our click-through rate is off then we're going to look at our creative we're going to look at our images we're going to look at our headline we're going to look at our our body copy and we're going to test new um creative if our media performance is where it's supposed to be right then we're going to look at price testing because right if we've got marketing performing the way it's supposed to media is performing the way it's supposed to then we're going to see if we can manipulate the cpa to aov ratio through pricing through increasing the value side through pricing if it is not an economic issue because we've got one or more marketing assets that are underperforming then what we're going to do is we're going to identify the campaign constraint the earliest underperforming stage and that is what we are then gonna focus our attention on when it comes to optimization so we're not gonna like in this instance right we're not gonna go back and try to create a new whiz-bang offer right that's just not the way that we approach it we're gonna focus on what is the constraint and we're going to see if we can fix the cpa to aov ratio by fixing the constraint by dialing in the constraint now the thing to really understand in terms of long-term thinking is that there will always be a constraint in every campaign like it's just like the analogy that i've used for years in in in teaching this and optimization is it's just like a chain link uh right every every chain uh has a weakest link at all times there is one weakest link and the strength of the chain is ultimately determined by the weakest link it's not determined by the strongest link in the chain it's determined it will snap when the weakest link comes under you know exceeds the the pressure that it can withstand and so once you take the weakest link and you strengthen it and you write you you reinforce it well now there is another weakest link that you can improve so from a long-term perspective this process if we all if we see like all right this thing is is performing where we want and now we want to optimize it well ongoing the optimization is looking at which piece is still the weakest even though they all may be strong they all may be performing well which one is the weakest one that we can uh that we can um address and so the high level recap of this is is it an economic issue or is it a performance issue before i dive in and i immediately start playing around with marketing assets and changing headlines and changing offers and changing leads and right re-leading a campaign and doing all this i want to start with right is it an economic issue or is it a performance issue if it's an economic issue is it a media performance like is the economic issue coming from the performance of our media or is it coming from just inadequate pricing right meaning like you know it it kind of reminds me like teddy i i don't know if somebody knows lindsay would probably know what a an ounce of gold costs right now right well an an ounce of gold well when i say that it's it's a pricing issue it's like you know let's say an ounce of gold what is an ounce of gold petty how much is an ounce of gold right now somebody know shout it out 1900 somewhere in that range 1900 it's 1900 so a pricing issue right a pricing issue is when like there's a 1900 asset that we're trying to acquire right but we're only we're we're like we're shopping around with a thousand dollars in our pocket and we're right the issue is not like that you can't acquire the asset the the issue is that what you're willing to trade like the issue is that the the equation doesn't match up and so if it's not performance of our media then we have to adjust our pricing to be able to pay for what the market has determined the traffic is worth so media performance is all about targeting and creative pricing issue is all about price testing right starting with the aov side or the um the ups the transaction maximization offer side if it's a performance issue it is all about just identify the step which is the campaign constraint the earliest step in the campaign which is the constraint this is how we go about taking our campaigns and making them gold now i don't want you to think that we can take every campaign and make it gold because that would not be accurate by any stretch but this is the approach the methodical approach that we take to optimization it's not just a matter of hearing a great idea and then running with it because you've heard that it works for somebody else and therefore let's plug it into your campaign i've seen so many marketers and entrepreneurs that have uh campaigns with a low sales conversion rate and they endlessly work on let's say the offer the offer the offer the offer they've got a vsl they work on the offer they offer they offer the offer and then when we do an analysis which we can do with a vsl a deeper analysis to see where folks are dropping off right we uncovered the fact that 95 plus percent of the people were never even making it to the offer right they had a problem with the lead and they had a problem with a part of the marketing argument therefore constantly working on the offer and trying to come up with a new offer new offer new offer new offer the reason why it wasn't fixing the problem was because they were they weren't addressing the problem because they didn't do an assessment that's what this methodical approach is all about it's about narrowing down what we need to focus on to make it gold

Not sure if this is the right place to ask, but…

Todd, how do you think Agora Financial has managed to sell their newsletters so successfully for so many years?

I’ve been following some of their funnels lately, and what puzzles me is — a lot of their acquisition campaigns are built around very specific market predictions and urgent calls to action tied to exact dates and events… which often don’t actually play out.

Plus, they often use nearly overblown promises. And yet, somehow, people keep buying.

Maybe you know how it works?

metadata.twitter:description:
"Todd Brown is THE expert other marketing experts turn to when they need help with their own marketing."

My channel is all about teaching you the most effective customer acquisition and customer monetization methods you can use to grow your business.

So, if you're an ambitious entrepreneur or marketer looking for the most effective direct response marketing strategies and tactics to scale big and fast, make sure to subscribe to my channel right now.

As you can see, my channel publishes no-fluff videos with proven in-the-trenches marketing nuggets so you get more new buyers, more sales, more profits, and more enjoyment from your business. 

If that sounds valuable for you, then make sure to join me by subscribing!

About Todd Brown
Considered the #1 authority on engineering profitable customer acquisition campaigns, Todd Brown is the marketing expert other marketing experts go to when they need help with their own business.

With clients in over 71 different countries, operating in over 65 different mass and niche markets, it’s been said that Todd has helped his clients and students engineer more six and seven-figure marketing campaigns than any other expert online today.

His list of coaching students, consulting clients, and subscribers reads like a Who’s Who of A-List Entrepreneurs.

His Agency has created the promotions behind some of the biggest direct response marketers and companies online today.

And, entrepreneurs fly-in to West Palm Beach, Florida — from all around the globe — for a single day of guidance with Todd to implement his renowned E5 Method.

# [THE Best Place to Start Running Ads for MAXIMUM Sales Funnel Success?](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6j3BIlvW-JQ)

**Visibility**: Public
**Uploaded by**: [Todd Brown](http://www.youtube.com/@ToddBrownMarketing)
**Uploaded at**: 2024-10-29T09:47:06-07:00
**Published at**: 2024-10-29T09:47:06-07:00
**Length**: 27:26
**Views**: 833
**Likes**: 29
**Category**: Howto & Style

## Description

```
When running ads online, choosing the correct platform for your product, audience, and offer can be even more important than the quality of your ad creative. 

This is why it's so important you understand the differences between platforms, their pros and cons, and the best place to start running ads to maximize your sales funnel success. 

Listen to Todd Brown and John Mulry discuss the process the E5 Agency uses to consistently identify the best platform to start running ads for any new ad campaign.

---
To learn more about having John launch, manage, and optimize your paid media, go to: http://e5agency.com
```
